mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic products in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the complexity of managing stateful applications.

steps.title: Add the Elastic Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install ECK.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the ECK Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for ECK to manage Elasticsearch and Kibana resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/all-in-one.yaml

```

> This command installs the CRDs required for ECK.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace.

```bash

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0

```

> Installs the ECK operator using Helm.

steps.title: Create an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a Custom Resource (CR).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

```

> Creates a 3-node Elasticsearch cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> This command lists the Elasticsearch resources in the 'elastic-system' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all managed Elasticsearch clusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing resources, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Backs up the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Elasticsearch cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch pods may not be in a running state due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n elastic-system' and ensure there are enough resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Elasticsearch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch service may not be reachable due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n elastic-system' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions may not be installed correctly or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/all-in-one.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account used by ECK may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct roles and bindings with 'kubectl get clusterrolebindings'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x and improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.13, ensure all Elasticsearch clusters are compatible with version 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system' showing your Elasticsearch cluster with a status of 'green'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
